--- title: "Spark NLP" engine: "jupyter" date: "2026-02-17" execute: echo: true eval: true warning: false message: false format: revealjs: scroll-view: true --- # Spark-NLP in perspective {background-color="#1c191c"} ## Spark NLP [Spark NLP](https://sparknlp.org) provides an example of an application in the Apache Spark Ecosystem . . . Spark NLP relies on the Spark SQL Lib and Spark Dataframes (high level APIs) and also on the Spark ML Lib. . . . Spark NLP borrows ideas from existing NLP softwares and adapts the known techniques to the Spark principles ... NLP deals with many applications of machine learning - Automatic translation (see [deepl.com](https://www.deepl.com/translator)) - Topic modeling (text clustering) - Sentiment Analysis - LLMs - ... # NLP Libraries {background-color="#1c191c"} ## Two flavors of NLP libraries - *Functionality* Libraries [nltk.org](https://www.nltk.org) - *Annotation* Libraries [spaCy's site](https://spacy.io) ## spaCy and Spark? A [databricks notebook](https://winf-hsos.github.io/databricks-notebooks/big-data-analytics/ss-2020/NLP%20with%20Python%20and%20spaCy%20-%20First%20Steps.html) discusses possible interactions between spaCy and Spark on a use case: . . . - Get the tweets (the texts) into a Spark dataframe using `spark.sql()` - Convert the Spark dataframe to a `numpy` array - Stream all tweets in batches using `nlp.pipe()` - Go through the processed tweets and take copy everything we need in a large array object - Convert back the large array object into a Spark dataframe - Save the dataframe as table, so we can query the whole thing withh SQL again . . . ::: callout-warning ### No hint at parallelizing spaCy's annotation process ::: ## spaCy v2 (current v3.7) > spaCy v2 now fully supports the `Pickle` protocol, making it easy to use spaCy with Apache Spark. [spaCy v2 documentation](https://spacy.io/usage/v2) # A short example (from [John Snow Labs](https://sparknlp.org)) {background-color="#1c191c"} ## Objectives - Initializing a `sparknlp` session - Building a toy NLP pipeline for detecting dates in a text ## Imports `sparknlp` and others ::: {#83bd8467 .cell execution_count=2} ``` {.python .cell-code} # Import Spark NLP from sparknlp.base import * from sparknlp.annotator import * from sparknlp.pretrained import PretrainedPipeline import sparknlp ``` ::: ## Initiate Spark session Assuming `standalone` mode on a laptop. `master` runs on `localhost` ::: {#spark-session-builder .cell execution_count=4} ``` {.python .cell-code} spark = SparkSession.builder \ .appName("Spark NLP") \ .master("local[*]") \ .config("spark.driver.memory", "16G") \ .config("spark.serializer", "org.apache.spark.serializer.KryoSerializer") \ .config("spark.kryoserializer.buffer.max", "2000M") \ .config("spark.driver.maxResultSize", "0") \ .config("spark.jars.packages", "com.johnsnowlabs.nlp:spark-nlp_2.12:6.3.2") \ .getOrCreate() ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout} ``` :: loading settings :: url = jar:file:/home/boucheron/.sdkman/candidates/spark/3.5.3/jars/ivy-2.5.1.jar!/org/apache/ivy/core/settings/ivysettings.xml ``` ::: ::: ## Check versions and context ::: {#81261407 .cell execution_count=5} ``` {.python .cell-code} sparknlp.version() ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-display execution_count=4} ``` '6.3.2' ``` ::: ::: ::: {#0c68fa2b .cell execution_count=6} ``` {.python .cell-code} spark.sparkContext ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-display execution_count=5} ```{=html}

SparkContext

Spark UI

Version
v3.5.3
Master
local[*]
AppName
Spark NLP
``` ::: ::: # Toy (big) data {background-color="#1c191c"} ## Toy big raw data ::: {#3cc3e9da .cell execution_count=7} ``` {.python .cell-code} fr_articles = [ ("Le dimanche 11 juillet 2021, Chiellini a utilisé le mot Kiricocho lorsque Saka s'est approché du ballon pour le penalty.",), ("La prochaine Coupe du monde aura lieu en novembre 2022.",), ("À Noël 800, Charlemagne se fit couronner empereur à Rome.",), ("Le Marathon de Paris a lieu le premier dimanche d'avril 2024",) ] ``` ::: ## Toy big data (cont'd) ::: {#790e045b .cell execution_count=8} ``` {.python .cell-code} articles_cols = ["text"] df = spark.createDataFrame( data=fr_articles, schema=articles_cols) df.printSchema() ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout} ``` root |-- text: string (nullable = true) ``` ::: ::: ## Pipelines ::: {#eb40f0dc .cell execution_count=9} ``` {.python .cell-code} document_assembler = ( DocumentAssembler() .setInputCol("text") .setOutputCol("document") ) ``` ::: ::: callout Column `document` contains the 'text' to be annotated as well as some possible metadata. Starting point of any annotation process Spark NLP relies on Spark SQL for storing, moving, data. ::: ## Looking for dates ::: {#d5642252 .cell execution_count=10} ``` {.python .cell-code} date_matcher = DateMatcher() \ .setInputCols(['document']) \ .setOutputCol("date") \ .setOutputFormat("MM/dd/yyyy") \ .setSourceLanguage("fr") ``` ::: ::: callout - Spark NLP adopts an original way of storing annotations - Spark NLP creates columns for annotations - Spark NLP stores annotation in Spark dataframes - Annotators are - Tranformers - Estimators - Models ::: ## Transformation/Action ::: {#0f478b47 .cell execution_count=11} ``` {.python .cell-code} assembled = ( document_assembler.transform(df) ) ``` ::: ## ::: {#b33b0e27 .cell execution_count=12} ``` {.python .cell-code} ( date_matcher .transform(assembled) .select('date') .show(10, False) ) ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout} ``` +-------------------------------------------------+ |date | +-------------------------------------------------+ |[{date, 10, 21, 07/11/2021, {sentence -> 0}, []}]| |[{date, 41, 53, 11/01/2022, {sentence -> 0}, []}]| |[] | |[{date, 3, 60, 01/01/2024, {sentence -> 0}, []}] | +-------------------------------------------------+ ``` ::: ::: ## More ::: {#more-challenges .cell execution_count=13} ``` {.python .cell-code} fr_articles.append(("Nous nous sommes rencontrés le 13/05/2018 puis le 18/05/2020.",)) fr_articles.append(("Nous nous sommes rencontrés il y a 2 jours et il m'a dit qu'il nous rendrait visite la semaine prochaine.",)) ``` ::: ## Rebuild the data ::: {#f712c52e .cell execution_count=14} ``` {.python .cell-code} df = spark.createDataFrame( data=fr_articles, schema=articles_cols) df.printSchema() df.show() ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout} ``` root |-- text: string (nullable = true) +--------------------+ | text| +--------------------+ |Le dimanche 11 ju...| |La prochaine Coup...| |À Noël 800, Charl...| |Le Marathon de Pa...| |Nous nous sommes ...| |Nous nous sommes ...| +--------------------+ ``` ::: ::: ::: {#788ed103 .cell execution_count=15} ``` {.python .cell-code} assembled = ( document_assembler.transform(df) ) ``` ::: ## Look for dates again ::: {#0c8b76d0 .cell execution_count=16} ``` {.python .cell-code} ( date_matcher .transform(assembled) .select('date') .show(10, False) ) ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout} ``` +-------------------------------------------------+ |date | +-------------------------------------------------+ |[{date, 10, 21, 07/11/2021, {sentence -> 0}, []}]| |[{date, 41, 53, 11/01/2022, {sentence -> 0}, []}]| |[] | |[{date, 3, 60, 01/01/2024, {sentence -> 0}, []}] | |[{date, 31, 40, 05/13/2018, {sentence -> 0}, []}]| |[{date, 84, 92, 03/21/2026, {sentence -> 0}, []}]| +-------------------------------------------------+ ``` ::: ::: ## Another annotator ::: {#another-date-matcher .cell execution_count=17} ``` {.python .cell-code} date_matcher_bis = MultiDateMatcher() \ .setInputCols(['document']) \ .setOutputCol("date") \ .setOutputFormat("MM/dd/yyyy") \ .setSourceLanguage("fr") ``` ::: ## Other findings ::: {#98ce67d3 .cell execution_count=18} ``` {.python .cell-code} ( date_matcher_bis .transform(assembled) .select("date") .show(10, False) ) ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout} ``` +--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+ |date | +--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+ |[{date, 11, 22, 07/11/2021, {sentence -> 0}, []}] | |[{date, 41, 53, 11/01/2022, {sentence -> 0}, []}] | |[] | |[{date, 53, 62, 04/01/2024, {sentence -> 0}, []}] | |[{date, 31, 40, 05/13/2018, {sentence -> 0}, []}, {date, 50, 59, 05/18/2020, {sentence -> 0}, []}]| |[{date, 28, 37, 03/12/2026, {sentence -> 0}, []}, {date, 80, 88, 03/21/2026, {sentence -> 0}, []}]| +--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+ ``` ::: ::: # Spark NLP Design {background-color="#1c191c"} ## SQL Lib and Dataframes ## ML Lib, Transformers and Estimators # Spark NLP Pipelines {background-color="#1c191c"} ## Getting a corpus : ETL ::: {#4f1d8663 .cell execution_count=19} ``` {.python .cell-code} # Warm up pattern = 'URL: http://www.nytimes.com/(?P[0-9]{4}/[0-9]{2}/[0-9]{2})/.*' title = 'URL: http://www.nytimes.com/[0-9]{4}/[0-9]{2}/[0-9]{2}/(.*)' reg_date = re.compile(pattern) reg_title = re.compile(title) ``` ::: ## Getting a corpus (cont'd) ::: {#getting-the-ny-data .cell execution_count=20} ``` {.python .cell-code} nypath = Path('../../data/nytimes_news_articles.txt') corpus_list = list() ``` ::: ## Building the corpus {.scrollable} ::: {#f95fde7e .cell execution_count=21} ``` {.python .cell-code} ## label: parsing-ny-corpus with open(nypath, encoding='UTF-8') as fd: doc, document = None, None while l := fd.readline(): if m := reg_date.match(l): if doc is not None: corpus_list.append((*document, doc)) doc, document = None, None ymd = date(*[int(n) for n in m.groups()[0].split('/')]) title = ( reg_title.match(l) .groups()[0] .split('/') ) document = (ymd, title[-1], '/'.join(title[:-1])) doc = '' else: doc = doc + l else: if doc is not None: corpus_list.append((*document, doc)) ``` ::: ## ::: {#building-df-from-corpus .cell execution_count=22} ``` {.python .cell-code} df_texts = spark.createDataFrame( corpus_list, schema= StructType([ StructField('date', DateType(), False), StructField('title', StringType(), False), StructField('topic', StringType(), False), StructField('text', StringType(), True) ])) ``` ::: ## Checking the loaded data ::: {#cell-check-corpus-df .cell execution_count=23} ``` {.python .cell-code} df_texts.printSchema() df_texts.count() ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout} ``` root |-- date: date (nullable = false) |-- title: string (nullable = false) |-- topic: string (nullable = false) |-- text: string (nullable = true) ``` ::: ::: {#check-corpus-df .cell-output .cell-output-display execution_count=22} ``` 8888 ``` ::: ::: ## Saving Locally ::: {#saving-to-parquet .cell execution_count=24} ``` {.python .cell-code} df_texts.write.parquet('../../data/ny_corpus_pq', mode="overwrite") ``` ::: ## Reloading ::: {#e1b6b62f .cell execution_count=25} ``` {.python .cell-code} spam = spark.read.parquet('../../data/ny_corpus_pq') spam.printSchema() ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout} ``` root |-- date: date (nullable = true) |-- title: string (nullable = true) |-- topic: string (nullable = true) |-- text: string (nullable = true) ``` ::: ::: ::: {#6109fd97 .cell execution_count=26} ``` {.python .cell-code} spam.rdd.getNumPartitions() ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-display execution_count=25} ``` 20 ``` ::: ::: ## Feeding the spark-nlp ::: {#22fd0105 .cell execution_count=27} ``` {.python .cell-code} corpus_assembled = ( document_assembler.transform(df_texts) ) ``` ::: ::: {#4762392e .cell execution_count=28} ``` {.python .cell-code} corpus_assembled.printSchema() ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout} ``` root |-- date: date (nullable = false) |-- title: string (nullable = false) |-- topic: string (nullable = false) |-- text: string (nullable = true) |-- document: array (nullable = true) | |-- element: struct (containsNull = true) | | |-- annotatorType: string (nullable = true) | | |-- begin: integer (nullable = false) | | |-- end: integer (nullable = false) | | |-- result: string (nullable = true) | | |-- metadata: map (nullable = true) | | | |-- key: string | | | |-- value: string (valueContainsNull = true) | | |-- embeddings: array (nullable = true) | | | |-- element: float (containsNull = false) ``` ::: ::: ## Looking for dates (again) ::: {#0e39f88c .cell execution_count=29} ``` {.python .cell-code} ( date_matcher_bis .transform(corpus_assembled) .select("title", "date") .show(10, False) ) ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout} ``` |washington-nationals-max-scherzer-baffles-mets-completing-a-sweep.html |[{date, 478, 1690, 01/04/2026, {sentence -> 0}, []}] | 3111, 05/30/2014, {sentence -> 0}, []}, {date, 1090, 1098, 03/07/2026, {sentence -> 0}, []}] | |latest-sign-of-change-in-harlem-its-congressional-candidate.html |[{date, 389, 1270, 12/01/2026, {sentence -> 0}, []}] | +-----------------------------------------------------------------------------+--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+ only showing top 10 rows ``` ::: ::: ::: {.callout-warning} Extracted dates should be taken with a grain of salt ::: ## Public pipelines ::: {#5ae23b3c .cell execution_count=30} ``` {.python .cell-code} from sparknlp.pretrained import PretrainedPipeline explain_document_pipeline = PretrainedPipeline("explain_document_ml") ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout} ``` explain_document_ml download started this may take some time. Approx size to download 9 MB [ | ] ``` ::: ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout} ``` explain_document_ml download started this may take some time. Approximate size to download 9 MB Download done! Loading the resource. [ / ] [ — ] [OK!] ``` ::: ::: ## Chaining annotators {.scrollable} ::: {#5563f59a .cell execution_count=31} ``` {.python .cell-code} sentenceDetector = SentenceDetector() \ .setInputCols(["document"]) \ .setOutputCol("sentence") regexTokenizer = Tokenizer() \ .setInputCols(["sentence"]) \ .setOutputCol("token") ``` ::: ::: {#82d0ce37 .cell execution_count=32} ``` {.python .cell-code} finisher = Finisher() \ .setInputCols(["token"]) \ .setIncludeMetadata(True) ``` ::: ## Chaining annotators (cont'd) {.scrollable} ::: {#7cc7ba15 .cell execution_count=33} ``` {.python .cell-code} pipeline = Pipeline().setStages([ document_assembler, sentenceDetector, regexTokenizer, finisher ]) ``` ::: ## Fitting and transforming ::: {#94543c5e .cell execution_count=34} ``` {.python .cell-code} spam = ( pipeline.fit(df_texts) .transform(df_texts) .select("finished_token") .collect() ) ``` ::: ## A customized pipeline ::: {#76f99efc .cell execution_count=35} ``` {.python .cell-code} stemmer = ( Stemmer() .setInputCols(['token']) .setOutputCol('stem') ) ``` ::: ::: {#f2ec29dc .cell execution_count=36} ``` {.python .cell-code} lemmatizer = ( LemmatizerModel.pretrained() .setInputCols(['token']) .setOutputCol('lemma') ) ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout} ``` lemma_antbnc download started this may take some time. ``` ::: ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout} ``` Approximate size to download 907.6 KB [ | ] ``` ::: ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout} ``` lemma_antbnc download started this may take some time. [Stage 34:===========================================> (3 + 1) / 4] [ | ] [OK!] ``` ::: ## ::: {#58aaea06 .cell execution_count=38} ``` {.python .cell-code} finisher = ( Finisher() .setInputCols([ 'token', # 'stem', # 'lemma', 'pos']) .setIncludeMetadata(False) .setOutputAsArray(True) ) ``` ::: ## ::: {#44593580 .cell execution_count=39} ``` {.python .cell-code} pipeline = ( Pipeline() .setStages([ document_assembler, sentenceDetector, regexTokenizer, posTagger, finisher ]) ) ``` ::: ## ::: {#41c84616 .cell execution_count=40} ``` {.python .cell-code} spam = ( pipeline.fit(df_texts) .transform(df_texts) .selectExpr("*") .collect() ) ``` ::: # Spark NLP and feature engineering {background-color="#1c191c"} ## Topic modelling ## TF-IDF ## Latent Dirichlet Allocation # Distributed computations {background-color="#1c191c"} ## Execution modes - standalone - client - cluster - # Spark NLP and composite types in Spark Dataframes ```{.python} >>> result = documentAssembler.transform(data) >>> result.select("document").show(truncate=False) +----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+ |document | +----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+ |[[document, 0, 51, Spark NLP is an open-source text processing library., [sentence -> 0], []]]| +----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------+ >>> result.select("document").printSchema() root |-- document: array (nullable = True) | |-- element: struct (containsNull = True) | | |-- annotatorType: string (nullable = True) | | |-- begin: integer (nullable = False) | | |-- end: integer (nullable = False) | | |-- result: string (nullable = True) | | |-- metadata: map (nullable = True) | | | |-- key: string | | | |-- value: string (valueContainsNull = True) | | |-- embeddings: array (nullable = True) | | | |-- element: float (containsNull = False) ``` Column `document` is of type `ArrayType()`. The basetype of `document` column is of `StructType()` (`element`), the `element` contains subfields of primitive type, but alo a field of type `map` (`MapType()`) and a field of type `StructType()`. ::: {#8ce97b8f .cell execution_count=41} ``` {.python .cell-code} spark.stop() ``` :::
